subject: Sociology
title: Bourdieu and Cultural Capital
description: A quick win: understand Bourdieu and Cultural Capital.

hook.text: Two equally smart students take the same test. One fails. The difference? What their parents talked about at dinner.

buildup.text: Pierre Bourdieu introduced cultural capital — the knowledge, tastes, and habits that schools reward.

discovery.text: Middle-class children arrive at school already speaking, thinking, and behaving in ways teachers value.

twist.text: Schools treat cultural capital as natural talent. They reward class advantages while calling them merit.

climax.text: Educational success depends not just on effort but on how much cultural capital your family already gave you.

quiz.question: What is cultural capital according to Bourdieu?
options: Knowledge, tastes, and habits that schools reward
